notes_for_human_reviewer: DELIBERATELY MIXED PROFILE — adapted from launchpad_content_package's Meridian version to the repo's healthcare scenario. Strong on differentiation (R/I/A/D categories used well, entries generally in the right bucket) and living-artifact (explicit v1.0/v1.1/v1.2/v1.3 history with dated status changes, closed items, additions). Weak on mitigation_realism (every mitigation is a platitude: 'Monitor closely', 'Follow up', 'Keep an eye on it', 'Prep dashboard', 'Coordinate', 'Plan ahead' — no specificity, no dates, no named actions). Weak on ownership (owners are generic roles: 'PM', 'Eng team', 'Project team', 'IT', 'Training lead' — no named people). Completeness is low-medium: triggers mostly missing, review dates absent, impact/likelihood present. The critical test: does the AI score each dimension on its own merits, or does it halo-average a mediocre overall? Tight tolerance is avoided on ownership because 'PM' vs 'named person' is a 2-or-3 judgment cell.
